highlights.title: Musk’s Trial Against OpenAI Hits Some Rough Spots in First Week
highlights.description: Elon Musk set out to tell a jury that his falling out with OpenAI was a simple tale of betrayal.

highlights.title: Uber wants to turn its millions of drivers into a sensor grid for self-driving companies
highlights.description: Praveen Neppalli Naga, Uber's chief technology officer, revealed the plan in an interview at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, describing it as a natural extension of a nascent program the company announced in late January called AV Labs.

highlights.title: Tesla launches Model 3 RWD in Canada at record-low $39,490 ($29,000 USD) from China
highlights.description:
Tesla has launched a new Model 3 Premium RWD trim in Canada starting at just $39,490 CAD — a record-low price for the electric sedan in the country. The price, which converts to roughly $29,000 USD, is made possible by sourcing the vehicle from Giga Shanghai instead of Fremont, California.
The move marks the first time Tesla is selling China-made vehicles in Canada since the country imposed a 100% tariff on Chinese EVs back in 2024 — and it creates a massive price gap with the Model 3 Performance.
more…

highlights.title: New Bitcoin quantum proposal offers Satoshi Nakamoto a way to prove control without moving BTC
highlights.description: A new design proposed by venture fund Paradigm would let holders privately timestamp proof that they control vulnerable keys before quantum computers arrive, creating a possible rescue path if Bitcoin ever sunsets old addresses.
